import {
  ColorScales,
  TypographySamples,
  RadiusSamples,
} from "./theme-demo";

export const metadata = { title: "Theme" };

# Theme & Design Tokens

A single stylesheet (`tokens.css`) defines every color, size, and motion value in Patch UI. Components read from it through Tailwind utilities. Light and dark switch from one `.dark` class on `<html>`. No JavaScript required.

The scheme is deliberately small: a pure-neutral gray scale re-hexed against Radix Colors Gray (light base `#fcfcfc`, dark base `#0a0a0a`), two surface families (`--layer-*` for lifted panels, `--fill-*` for tinted chips), four ink tiers, four hairline steps (alpha), three fixed semantic status roles, one monochrome primary. Consumers layer their own brand by redefining `--primary` in a single downstream layer — no separate accent slot ships.

## Colors

<ColorScales />

**Base + layers.** The page sits on `--base` — `#fcfcfc` in light, deep near-black `#0a0a0a` in dark (below Radix gray 1 to avoid banding on 6-bit OLED panels). `--layer-1` is the default lifted panel: elevated Card, Modal, Menu popup, Combobox popup, Tooltip, Select popup, Input, Table container, secondary Button chrome. Direction is Radix-native: in light `--layer-1` (`#f9f9f9`, gray 2) is a subtly darker card rung; in dark it lifts a subtle rung above near-black base. `--layer-2` (`#f0f0f0` / `#1c1c1c`) is a row/button-rest step above that.

**Fills.** `--fill-1` and `--fill-2` are tinted chip fills: Toggle track, Badge neutral, Switch off-track, skeleton block, striped table rows, active pill Tab, menu item highlight. In the Phase 1 preview `--fill-1` collapses onto the same rung as `--layer-2` (both gray 3); the token names stay live so callsites keep resolving. `--fill-2` is the stronger active-state indication.

**Interaction-state overlays.** `--layer-hover` and `--layer-selected` are alpha overlays (Radix grayA 3 / grayA 5) that any transparent-at-rest control reaches for: `--layer-hover` on `hover:` and `focus-visible:`, `--layer-selected` on transient press (`active:`) and selected rows / nav items. Held states — an open popup's trigger (`data-[popup-open]:`) and a Toggle switched on (`data-[pressed]:`) — use `bg-hairline-strong` instead: the fill takes the hover border color and bordered variants drop to `border-transparent` (alpha would double-stack where border overlaps fill), so the control reads as one solid chip while it's held. Because they darken whatever surface sits underneath rather than paint over it, the state reads on base, `--layer-1`, `--fill-1`, or a consumer's own background without disappearing. `--layer-selected` (~12%) is ~2x the hover strength (~6%) so pressed always reads visibly darker than hover.

**Ink (text).** Four tiers, pure gray, no chroma. `--ink` is primary text (headings, body) at Radix gray 12. `--ink-muted` is meta text and default icon tone at gray 11. `--ink-subtle` is placeholder and "missing value" copy. `--ink-tertiary` is disabled and footnote. In the Phase 1 preview `--ink-subtle` and `--ink-tertiary` both resolve to gray 10 (`#838383` / `#7b7b7b`); the token names stay alive but they render identically.

**Semantic status.** `--error`, `--warning`, `--success` are fixed hex, identical in both themes so a warning always reads as a warning. Each has `-hover`, `-active`, and `-fg` variants; the `-fg` token carries label color so fill and text invert together.

**Hairlines.** Four tiers of alpha (Radix grayA 3 / 6 / 7 / 8) so a border on a nested card reads the same perceived weight as one on base — solid hex compounds when stacked, alpha doesn't. `--hairline-soft` (grayA 3, ~6% alpha) is a whisper-weight edge for Card `elevated` and other surfaces where the border should be present but recede. `--hairline` (grayA 6) is the standard input/menu/divider weight. `--hairline-strong` (grayA 7) and `--hairline-tertiary` (grayA 8) are hover-state emphases.

**Primary (monochrome).** `--primary` resolves to `--ink` and `--on-primary` to `--base`. No chromatic brand ships. `--primary-hover` and `--primary-active` are computed via `color-mix` toward `--base`, so a consumer's brand override on `--primary` automatically produces matching hover/active steps.

## Typography

Two families cover every use: **Inter** (variable) for everything that isn't code, **JetBrains Mono** for code. Size and weight are separate axes rather than a compound recipe.

**Size.** Eight tokens: `text-micro` (11), `text-mini` (12), `text-small` (14), `text-regular` (16, default body), `text-large` (18), `text-title3` (20), `text-title2` (24), `text-title1` (36).

**Weight.** Five tokens exposed as Tailwind utilities: `font-light` (300), `font-normal` (450, default body), `font-medium` (500), `font-semibold` (600), `font-bold` (700). Body defaults to 450, headings default to 500, so most call sites don't need an explicit weight.

**Line height.** Set on the element, not on the size utility. Body inherits 1.5, `<p>` uses 1.7, `<h1>–<h6>` use 1.25. A size utility on a `<div>` picks up 1.5 from body; wrapping the same content in `<p>` opens it up to 1.7.

<TypographySamples />

Rules of thumb:
- Reach for a size + weight pair (`text-small font-medium`) rather than inventing new compound tokens.
- Never `text-[Npx]` inline. If a size is missing, add it to the scale.
- Never `font-mono` for decoration. Reserve for `code`, `pre`, `kbd`, install snippets, and hex values shown as code.

## Sizing and motion

**Radius.** Seven values: `--radius-4/6/8/10/12/16/full`.

<RadiusSamples />

`radius-4` is reserved for very small controls (Checkbox, radio thumb) where 6px reads too round. `radius-6` for badges/tags and small chip-scale elements. `radius-8` for controls (Button `square`, Input, Select, Textarea, Toggle `square`, Tooltip, ModalInset). `radius-12` for floating panels (Menu popup, Combobox popup, Select popup, Popover, Command, Sheet, Modal, Calendar). `radius-16` for marketing chrome (hero panels). `radius-full` for pill Buttons, Avatars, and circular icon buttons.

**Shadows.** Elevation is intentional and sparing. `--shadow-card` (elevated Card + slider/switch thumbs), `--shadow-menu` (Menu / Combobox / Select popup), `--shadow-tooltip` (Tooltip), `--shadow-modal` (Modal / Sheet / Toast). Card `variant="elevated"` applies `shadow-card` in light and `dark:shadow-none` — the token has a dark value for slider/switch thumbs that need to remain visible against their track.

**Motion.** One easing (`--ease-standard`), two durations: `--duration-state` (75ms, for hover/active transitions) and `--duration-overlay` (150ms, for open/close). Under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, both collapse to `0ms` automatically.

**Focus.** A 2px outline offset 2px outside the element border, colored `--primary` so it inherits any consumer brand override. Delivered through the `focusRing` recipe. Inputs are the intentional exception: text fields match `:focus-visible` on every click, so instead of the outside ring their border shifts to `--primary` plus a soft low-alpha halo hugging it (`--focus-halo`, 3px at 18% alpha, also `--primary`-derived).

## Customizing

Every token is a plain CSS variable. Override in your own CSS after importing the tokens and every component picks up the change at runtime, no rebuild required:

```css
/* app/globals.css */
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "./styles/patch-ui-tokens.css";

/* Ship a lavender brand: every primary Button, active tab, focus
   ring, and slider indicator picks it up automatically. */
:root {
  --primary: #8b5cf6;
  --on-primary: #ffffff;
}

.dark {
  --primary: #a78bfa;
  --on-primary: #0f0f10;
}
```

If you edit tokens that affect readable text, run `npm run check:contrast -w packages/react` to verify the [contrast contract](/docs/contrast) still holds.
